perturbation.original_text: "Documentation" means written information (whether contained in user or technical manuals, training materials, specifications or otherwise) regarding the Software and made available by Cisco in any manner (including on CD-Rom, or on-line). Customer's license to use the Software shall be limited to, and Customer shall not use the Software in excess of, a single hardware chassis or card or that number of agent(s), concurrent users, sessions, IP addresses, port(s), seat(s), server(s) or site(s), as set forth in the applicable Purchase Order which has been accepted by Cisco and for which Customer has paid to Cisco the required license fee.
perturbation.changed_text: "Documentation" means written information (whether contained in user or technical manuals, training materials, specifications or otherwise) regarding the Software and made available by Cisco in any manner (including on CD-Rom, or on-line). Customer's license to use the Software shall not be limited and Customer can use the software on as many hardware chassis or card or that number of agent(s), concurrent users, sessions, IP addresses, port(s), seat(s), server(s) or site(s), even not being set forth in the applicable Purchase Order which has been accepted by Cisco and regardless if Customer has paid to Cisco the required license fee.
perturbation.explanation: By modifying the definition of 'Documentation', the original clause limits software usage based on the purchase order terms and license fees paid. The changed text removes these limitations, allowing unlimited usage regardless of purchase order or payment. This directly contradicts the original intention of the software license agreement, creating uncertainty about the enforceable limits of software use.
perturbation.contradicted_text: License. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Cisco Systems, Inc. or its subsidiary licensing the Software instead of Cisco Systems, Inc. ("Cisco"), grants to Customer a nonexclusive and nontransferable license to use for Customer's internal business purposes the Software and the Documentation for which Customer has paid the required license fees. "Documentation" means written information (whether contained in user or technical manuals, training materials, specifications or otherwise) regarding the Software and made available by Cisco in any manner (including on CD-Rom, or on-line). Customer's license to use the Software shall not be limited and Customer can use the software on as many hardware chassis or card or that number of agent(s), concurrent users, sessions, IP addresses, port(s), seat(s), server(s) or site(s), even not being set forth in the applicable Purchase Order which has been accepted by Cisco and regardless if Customer has paid to Cisco the required license fee. Unless otherwise expressly provided in the Documentation, Customer shall use the Software solely as embedded in, for execution on, or (where the applicable documentation permits installation on non-Cisco equipment) for communication with Cisco equipment owned or leased by Customer.
perturbation.justification: The original text in the 'Documentation' definition limits software usage based on purchase order terms and paid license fees. The changed text removes these limitations, permitting unlimited use regardless of the purchase order or payment. This directly contradicts the license grant outlined in the 'Contradicted Text' section, creating a significant inconsistency about the enforceable limitations of software use. The change conflicts with the condition that the customer's license is dependent on having paid the required license fees.

perturbation.original_text: General Limitations. Except as otherwise expressly provided under this Agreement, Customer shall have no right, and Customer specifically agrees not to: (i) transfer, assign or sublicense its license rights to any other person or entity, or use the Software on unauthorized or secondhand Cisco equipment, and Customer acknowledges that any attempted transfer, assignment, sublicense or use shall be void; (ii) make error corrections to or otherwise modify or adapt the Software or create derivative works based upon the Software, or permit third parties to do the same; (iii) decompile, decrypt, reverse engineer, disassemble or otherwise reduce the Software to human- readable form; or (iv) use or permit the Software to be used to perform services for third parties without the express written authorization of Cisco.
perturbation.changed_text: General Limitations. Except as otherwise expressly provided under this Agreement, Customer shall have the right, and Customer specifically agrees to: (i) transfer, assign or sublicense its license rights to any other person or entity, or use the Software on unauthorized or secondhand Cisco equipment, and Customer acknowledges that any attempted transfer, assignment, sublicense or use shall be valid; (ii) make error corrections to or otherwise modify or adapt the Software or create derivative works based upon the Software, or permit third parties to do the same; (iii) decompile, decrypt, reverse engineer, disassemble or otherwise reduce the Software to human- readable form; or (iv) use or permit the Software to be used to perform services for third parties without the express written authorization of Cisco.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall have no right' to 'shall have the right' and negating the subsequent 'not' for each of the limitations (i-iv), the original restrictions on the customer's use of the software are directly contradicted. The original text explicitly forbids transferring the license, modifying the software, reverse engineering, and using it for third-party services. The modified text now explicitly permits these actions. This reversal introduces significant uncertainty about the actual limitations placed on the customer, directly contradicting the intended restrictions of the license agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: General Limitations. Except as otherwise expressly provided under this Agreement, Customer shall have the right, and Customer specifically agrees to: (i) transfer, assign or sublicense its license rights to any other person or entity, or use the Software on unauthorized or secondhand Cisco equipment, and Customer acknowledges that any attempted transfer, assignment, sublicense or use shall be valid; (ii) make error corrections to or otherwise modify or adapt the Software or create derivative works based upon the Software, or permit third parties to do the same; (iii) decompile, decrypt, reverse engineer, disassemble or otherwise reduce the Software to human- readable form; or (iv) use or permit the Software to be used to perform services for third parties without the express written authorization of Cisco. To the extent required by law, and at Customer's written request, Cisco shall provide Customer with the interface information needed to achieve interoperability between the Software and another independently created program, on payment of Cisco's Source: SCANSOURCE, INC., 10-K, 8/22/2019 Exhibit 10.38 applicable fee, if any. Customer shall observe strict obligations of confidentiality with respect to such information.
perturbation.justification: The modified text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text explicitly prohibits the customer from transferring licenses, modifying the software, reverse engineering, and using it for third-party services, stating they 'shall have no right'. The changed text permits these actions, stating they 'shall have the right'. This creates a direct conflict and uncertainty about the limitations, directly contradicting the terms.

perturbation.original_text: Term and Termination. This Agreement and the license granted herein shall remain effective until terminated. Customer may terminate this Agreement and the license at any time by destroying all copies of Software including any Documentation. Customer's rights under this Agreement will terminate immediately without notice from Cisco if Customer fails to comply with any provision of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Term and Termination. This Agreement and the license granted herein shall remain effective until terminated. Customer may terminate this Agreement and the license at any time without destroying all copies of Software including any Documentation. Customer's rights under this Agreement will not terminate immediately without notice from Cisco even if Customer fails to comply with any provision of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause specifies that the customer can terminate the agreement by destroying all copies of the software and that Cisco can terminate the agreement immediately if the customer fails to comply with any of the agreement's provisions. The modified version changes these conditions. First, it says the customer can terminate without destroying all copies of the software, and second, it states that the agreement does not terminate even if the customer fails to comply. This creates an in-text contradiction on the conditions for termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term and Termination. This Agreement and the license granted herein shall remain effective until terminated. Customer may terminate this Agreement and the license at any time without destroying all copies of Software including any Documentation. Customer's rights under this Agreement will not terminate immediately without notice from Cisco even if Customer fails to comply with any provision of this Agreement. Upon termination, Customer shall destroy all copies of Software and Documentation in its possession or control. Customer Records. Customer grants to Cisco and its independent accountants the right to examine Customer's books, records and accounts during Customer's normal business hours to verify compliance with this Agreement. In the event such audit discloses non-compliance with this Agreement, Customer shall promptly pay to Cisco the appropriate license fees.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states that the agreement will terminate immediately if the customer fails to comply with any provision. The changed text states that the agreement will not terminate immediately even if the customer fails to comply with any provision. The contradicted text is the changed text, and the contradiction exists within the same text. Since the changed text has two conflicting conditions for termination (destroying copies and compliance), and these conditions contradict each other, there is a strong in-text contradiction.
